title: Verilator internal error when --trace is enabled
body:
Hello, I've recently run into the following:
When Verilating with --trace (or --trace-fst) I encounter an Internal Error, however when I remove trace flags from the Verilator command line, verilation completes successfully.

The specific internal error:
```
- V3Expand.cpp:961:   expandAll: 
%Error: Internal Error: ../hal2/deps/pulp_axi/src/axi_dw_downsizer.sv:754:23: ../V3Expand.cpp:375: extending larger thing into smaller?
-node: EXTEND 0x60001a655290 <e7257064> {l754ax} u1=0x1 @dt=0x600016b61440@(w32)
```

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  

This is the specific line in the RTL.  

```
  754 |                 w_data[b + mst_port_offset - slv_port_offset]         = slv_req_i.w.data[8*b +: 8];
      |                       ^
                        ../hal2/deps/pulp_axi/src/axi_dw_converter.sv:101:1: ... note: In file included from 'axi_dw_converter.sv'
                        dw_test.sv:47:1: ... note: In file included from 'dw_test.sv'
```
The statement is inside an always_comb process where 'b' is an integer loop variable, mst_port_offset, and slv_port_offset are slices of a logic vector..

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

`/opt/verilator.20250424/bin/verilator_bin_dbg --binary --top dw_test -I../hal2/deps/pulp_axi/include -I../hal2/deps/pulp_axi/src -I../hal2/deps/pulp_platform/common_cells/include -I../hal2/deps/pulp_platform/common_cells/src ../hal2/deps/pulp_platform/common_cells/src/cf_math_pkg.sv ../hal2/deps/pulp_axi/src/axi_pkg.sv -Wno-WIDTH -Wno-UNSIGNED --trace-fst --debug dw_test.sv`

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

`Verilator 5.035 devel rev v5.034-175-g6ac2aa2d9`

What OS and distribution are you using?

`Darwin easyemachine.local 24.3.0 Darwin Kernel Version 24.3.0: Thu Jan  2 20:24:22 PST 2025; root:xnu-11215.81.4~3/RELEASE_ARM64_T6041 arm64`

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

I realize this isn't the best bug report but I have yet to get sv-bugpoint working to get a minimal subset.
Update: sv-bugpoint's minimized subset was essentially the same as the original set of RTL.  Will try and do something by hand....

Thanks.

-node: EXTEND 0x60001a655290 <e7257064> {l754ax} u1=0x1 @dt=0x600016b61440@(w32)
  754 |                 w_data[b + mst_port_offset - slv_port_offset]         = slv_req_i.w.data[8*b +: 8];
      |                       ^
                        ../hal2/deps/pulp_axi/src/axi_dw_converter.sv:101:1: ... note: In file included from 'axi_dw_converter.sv'
                        dw_test.sv:47:1: ... note: In file included from 'dw_test.sv'
